title: What’s the Deal with Margin Collapse? | Jonathan HarrellAn element with a bottom margin of 30px followed by a sibling element with a top margin of 30px will have their margins collapsed into a total margin of 30px between them.A parent element with a bottom border or bottom padding and a bottom margin of 30px will not collapse with its last child element’s bottom margin of 30px.An element with a bottom margin of 30px and its last child element with a bottom margin of 30px will collapse so that the child element’s margin collapses with that of its parent.An element that doesn’t take up space in the flow of the page will have its top and bottom margins collapse to 0.Certain attributes will cause an element and its first and last child element margins to not collapse.A parent element with a top border or top padding and a top margin of 30px will not collapse with its first child element’s top margin of 30px.A parent element with a top border or top padding and a top margin of 30px will not collapse with its first child element’s top margin of 30px.
